scenario_config.name: endurance_steady
_dataset_notes: national_medium scale — 1,000 students, 8 weeks (6 cold + 2 hot).
The dataset itself is not the point of this scenario; the point is that
the steady-state ingest rate from docs/research/ingest-capacity-model.md §7
(medium adoption: ~11 ingest RPS) can be sustained over a long window
without backend memory creep, buffer growth, or tail-latency drift.
_traffic_notes: Pure-ingest endurance run at the NFR-1 target rate (11 RPS for 15 min).
constant-arrival-rate executor (ramp_mode is unset) so the rate is flat
for the full window — this is an endurance test, not a ramp.
- zero dropped iterations across the full window,
- p95 < 1000 ms across the full window,
- http_req_duration p95 trend (no walk-up over 15 min),
- dropped_iterations omitted == zero (per §10.4 of the plan).
resource_sample_interval_seconds=10 keeps the docker-stats trace at a
manageable size over a 15-minute window while still resolving any drift.
Reader classes are all 0 — interactions with read load are covered by
